paper_title: Environmental Impact Assessment in Abraão Stream

methods.description: Process for collecting physical, chemical, and biological samples from the Abraão stream to assess environmental conditions and biodiversity.

steps.description: Three sampling sites were established in the Abraão stream.
steps.searchable_summary: establish sampling sites along the stream

steps.description: Samples were obtained during the dry season, considered ideal for ecological studies in streams.

The physical and chemical variables as pH, water temperature, the concentration of dissolved oxygen by the Winkler method, as well as the total nitrogen and phosphorus according to the Standard Methods, were determined for each sampling site.
steps.searchable_summary: measure physical and chemical variables at each site

steps.description: Sampling of Chironomidae larvae was carried out by 10 sub-samples arbitrarily taken using Surber sampler with 0.225 mm of mesh size and an area of 0.1 m².
steps.searchable_summary: collect Chironomidae larvae using Surber sampler

steps.description: The material was fixed in 5% formalin.
Returning to the laboratory, the larvae were sorted, identified at a stereomicroscope and preserved in 70% ethanol.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and identify larvae, preserve in ethanol

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data using a truncated lognormal distribution model to assess taxa equity and diversity.

steps.description: We used the truncate lognormal distribution based on the general equation described below.
We fitted the lognormal distribution to the equation based on a nonlinear regression of the datasets, and using least squares as loss function in an iterative quasi-Newton procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: fit lognormal distribution using nonlinear regression
